mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly payroll growth signals robust labor market conditions that support corporate earnings and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k in the prior month.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in unemployment rate above 4.6% indicates deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger risk-off sentiment, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE increases by >0.2pp in a month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) and housing starts (HOUST) signal weak demand conditions that compress corporate pricing power, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL declines >0.5% and HOUST declines >10% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals an accommodative monetary policy stance that lowers discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 40% of instances where FEDFUNDS declines below 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 105.0 signals strengthening manufacturing activity that boosts corporate profits, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO increases by >1.0% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in real sales (RSAFS) and consumer confidence (CPIAUCSL) indicates weakening household demand that pressures corporate revenue, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of periods where RSAFS declines >2% and CPIAUCSL declines >0.3% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% combined with stagnant GDP growth (GDPC1) signals stagflationary pressures that increase equity risk premiums, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5% and GDPC1 growth remains below 1.0% year-over-year.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 104.0 combined with rising oil prices (CPIAUCSL) indicates supply-side shocks that increase corporate costs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of periods where INDPRO declines >0.5% and CPIAUCSL increases >0.4% month-over-month.
